subject: VISUAL ART
preamble: The syllabus is intended to provide candidates with the opportunity to demonstrate the extent of their aesthetic awareness, emotional and visual development through self-participatory creative activities.
objectives: Assess candidates' development of deep sense of observation, analytical and expressive skills through a variety of self-participatory art activities.
Assess candidates' knowledge and skills in the use and maintenance of art tools, equipment and materials.
Assess candidates' level of appreciation of values and qualities of different works of art, including indigenous material culture.
Assess candidates' understanding and creative application of design elements and principles.
Assess candidates' knowledge of the history and branches of Art.
Assess candidates' knowledge and appreciation of the relationship between African Art and Western Art.
Assess candidates' understanding of the meaning, significance and role of art in socio-economic development and self-reliance.
Assess candidates' understanding and application of indigenous and modern technology in art.
Assess candidates' creative skills in the improvisation of local materials and resources.
Assess candidates' understanding of the relevance of art in other subjects areas.
Assess candidates' level of exposure to careers in art.

examination_scheme.summary: The examination consists of three papers: Papers 1, 2, and 3 all of which must be taken. Papers 1 and 2 will be combined in a composite paper and will be taken at one sitting.

papers.title: Multiple Choice Objective Test
papers.details: This paper will contain forty multiple choice objective questions to be taken in 50 minutes for 40 marks.

papers.title: Essay Paper
papers.details: Will consist of three sections: Sections A, B and C. Section A: General knowledge in art; Section B: Art of West Africa; Section C: Pre-historic art, Ancient Egyptian art and Western Art. The paper will contain a total of eight essay questions. Candidates will be required to answer four questions choosing one question from Section A, two from Section B and one question in Section C.

papers.title: Practical Test
papers.marks: 300 marks total (100 marks per section attempted)
papers.details: The paper shall have three sections: Sections A, B and C. Candidates for the May/June diet shall answer questions in Section A and those in either Section B or C. Those for the November/December diet shall answer questions in Sections A and B only. Section A (Drawing): 3 hours, 100 marks. Section B (2-Dimensional Creative Design): 3 hours, 100 marks. Section C (3-Dimensional Creative Design): Project executed within 6 months, 100 marks.

topics.title: GENERAL KNOWLEDGE IN ART
details: (a) Visual awareness, understanding and appreciation of Art elements, their forms, characteristics and functions e.g. line, colour, space, shape, form, texture, etc.
(b) Nature and branches of Art and careers in art.
(c) Principles of Art - creative application of art and design principles e.g. balance, rhythm, proportion, harmony, emphasis, variety, etc.
(d) Perspective - meaning and type e.g. aerial, linear (angular/parallel) and foreshortening.
(e) The knowledge of production, use and care of art/craft tools and materials e.g. brush, pencil, colour, palette, easels, etc.
(f) Art and craft terms e.g. tie and dye, biscuit ware, silhouette, relief, chiaroscuro, sfumato, aperture, etc.
(g) The inter-relationships of the arts e.g. music, visual art, dance, drama and literary arts.

topics.title: ART OF WEST AFRICA
details: Knowledge of traditional Art of West Africa, the basic concepts (e.g. animism, fetishism and mythology): art forms, geographical location, characteristics, underlying beliefs and the sacred and secular functions of art. A general knowledge of contemporary artists and art educator, their works, style, media, materials and contribution to the development of art.

subsections.subtitle: Ancient Traditional Art
details: Cameroon: Bamileke
Dahomey (Now Benin Republic): Fon
Gambia: Wollof, Mandinka, Jola
Ghana (Formerly Gold Coast): Fante, Ewe, Frafra, Ashanti
Guinea: Kissi
Cote d'Ivoire (Ivory Coast): Senufo, Baule
Liberia: Dan and Ngere
Mali: Dogon, Bambara
Sierra Leone: Mende, Sherbro
Upper Volta (Now Burkina Faso): Mossi, Bobo, Kurumba
Nigeria: Benin, Ife, Nok, Igbo-Ukwu, Esie, Owo, etc.

subsections.subtitle: Modern Traditional Art
details: Oshogbo art, Ashanti and Ewe Kente, Modern Benin art, Winneba pottery, Abuja pottery, Ntoso adinkra, Bida brass works, calabash carving, Enyiresi basketry, Mbari mud sculpture, weaving, leatherwork, wall decoration, indigenous Decorative Motifs.

subsections.subtitle: Contemporary Art
details: (a) Art institutions (art schools, art galleries, arts centres, museums). Various departments responsible for art and culture, Art organizations, e.g. INSEA, NSIAD, Ghana Artists Association, Ghana Craftsmen Association, Ghana Arts Council, GAT, NAAC, SNA, NSEA, Ona Art Movement, Ulli Art Movement, The Eye Society.
(b) Cultural festivals (their artistic significance e.g. costumes, symbols, objects, masks, body decorations, wall decorations, etc.).
(c) Outstanding contemporary West African Artists: their training, style, achievements and contributions to the development of art.

topics.title: PRE-HISTORIC, ANCIENT EGYPTIAN AND WESTERN ART
details: (a) Pre-historic (Earlyman's Art 20,000 - 2000 BC) (Paleolithic, Mesolithic and Neolithic stone Ages) beliefs, materials, characteristics and functions.
(b) Ancient Egyptian art 11,000 - 7,000 BC (Old, Middle and New Kingdoms) beliefs, styles and functions.
(c) Greek Art: Periods (Archaic, Classical and Hellenistic), characteristics and artists.
(d) Medieval Art: Features, media and characteristics.
(e) Renaissance Art: Early, Low and High Renaissance Art - Artists: Giotto, Donatello, Masaccio, etc.
(f) 19th Century Art: Impressionism (Paul Cezanne, Claude Monet, Edouard Manet, Georges Seurat); Post Impressionism (Vincent van Gogh, Paul Gauguin, etc.).
(g) 20th Century Art: Cubism: Influence of African Art and Artists - Pablo Picasso, Georges Braque.

topics.title: PART A - DRAWING
details: Tests candidates' ability to observe, analyse and accurately depict natural and man-made objects, as well as human structure and forms. Works rendered in pencil, pastel, charcoal, pen and ink, or poster/water colour.
(a) Drawing from Objects: Composition of arranged man-made objects (e.g., radio sets, car parts, bicycles, machines, bottles).
(b) Drawing from Nature: Study of natural objects (e.g., branches, flowers, foliage, fruits, vegetables, rocks, bones, insects, birds, shells, fish, crustaceans, skeletons).
(c) Drawing from Life: Accurate depiction of structure and form of a living model (male or female) in specified poses.

topics.title: PART B - CREATIVE DESIGN (2-DIMENSIONAL ART)
details: Tests candidates' ability to visualize ideas, originality, and technical proficiency in 2-Dimensional art forms.

subsections.subtitle: Graphic Design
details: (i) Lettering and Poster Design: Formal lettering (Roman, Gothic), pictorial posters, greeting cards, invitations, book jackets, logotypes, emblems, symbols, labels, wall hangings.
(ii) Book Illustration: Story and text illustrations for books, magazines, and newspapers.
(iii) Printmaking: Linocut, woodcut, yam print, stenciling, screen printing, etc.
(iv) Package Design: Wrappers, cereal packages, CD jackets, etc.
(v) Computer Graphics: Designing any graphic design areas using software (e.g., CorelDraw, Adobe Photoshop).

subsections.subtitle: Textile Design
details: Designing dyed or printed material (at least 2 metres long, unsewn) via batik, tie-and-dye, block, or screen printing.
(i) Appliqué: Shaped fabric pieces sewn on foundation fabric.
(ii) Tapestry: Fabric with a woven pattern/picture for wall hanging or upholstery.

subsections.subtitle: Picture-Making
details: (i) Painting: Illustrative composition of themes from everyday life or imagination, including Murals.
(ii) Photography: Basic knowledge and skills in shooting and printing portraits, landscapes, seascapes, nature, pets, and experimental photo tricks (photograms, image distortion).
(iii) Mosaic: Pictures made with small regular-shaped coloured materials (glass, paper, tile).
(iv) Collage: Pictures synthesized using irregular materials (cloth, paper, objects).

topics.title: PART C - CREATIVE DESIGN (3-DIMENSIONAL ART)
details: Project executed within 6 months using materials like clay, papier-mâché, wood, cement, fiberglass, POP, paperboards, etc. Notes, diagrams, and working drawings constitute 10% of total marks.

subsections.subtitle: Sculpture
details: Rendered in clay, metal, wood, papier-mâché, cement, etc. All clay works must be fired.

subsections.subtitle: Product Design/Modeling
details: Designing and producing models of industrial products (automobiles, phones, bottles, cosmetics).

subsections.subtitle: Ceramics
details: Ceramic wares (jugs, flower vases, bowls, toy animals/figures) moulded, hand-built, or wheel-thrown. Clay works must be fired.

subsections.subtitle: Crafts
details: (i) Basketry: Mats, chairs, hats, stools, bags, macramé woven/plaited in cane, raffia, etc.
(ii) Jewellery: Construction of ornaments with metals, beads, plastic, shells, seeds, etc.
(iii) Calabash Decoration: Designing and decorating calabash using various methods.
